tweets.title: Moving forward, GE Aerospace has assured HAL that it will supply an additional 22 engines by the end of the year to help clear the backlog. If “year” refers to the financial year, then together with the seven engines already delivered, HAL would have enough engines for 29 Tejas Mk1A aircraft, equivalent to more than 1.5 fighter squadrons ready for delivery, assuming production progresses as planned.
tweets.content:
Moving forward, GE Aerospace has assured HAL that it will supply an additional 22 engines by the end of the year to help clear the backlog.

If “year” refers to the financial year, then together with the seven engines already delivered, HAL would have enough engines for 29 Tejas Mk1A aircraft, equivalent to more than 1.5 fighter squadrons ready for delivery, assuming production progresses as planned.

tweets.title: This will be the fourth time this government has failed to contain very manageable violence inside the country, that too in the national capital every single time. - The CAA/NRC protests turned violent without much ground. - Shaheen Bagh turned into the Delhi riots. - The farmers' protest turned into the most audacious dash up to the Red Fort. And now, this CJP protest. I keep seeing the same faces that I have seen before every violent protest. Nothing happens to anyone. This protest, violence, and anger among the youth will start costing the ruling party dearly in the coming times. But apart from the electoral impact, the sheer incompetence in managing very simple situations, maintaining positive optics, and keeping anarchist elements in check is astonishing. It is funny that managing these things is apparently so hard for the government that they end up beating students without effectively communicating to them what they are doing wrong. Good luck with this approach.
tweets.content:
This will be the fourth time this government has failed to contain very manageable violence inside the country, that too in the national capital every single time.

- The CAA/NRC protests turned violent without much ground.

- Shaheen Bagh turned into the Delhi riots.

- The farmers' protest turned into the most audacious dash up to the Red Fort.

And now, this CJP protest.

I keep seeing the same faces that I have seen before every violent protest. Nothing happens to anyone.

This protest, violence, and anger among the youth will start costing the ruling party dearly in the coming times.

But apart from the electoral impact, the sheer incompetence in managing very simple situations, maintaining positive optics, and keeping anarchist elements in check is astonishing.

It is funny that managing these things is apparently so hard for the government that they end up beating students without effectively communicating to them what they are doing wrong.

Good luck with this approach.
tweets.author: @elitepredatorss

tweets.title: Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”. With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.content:
Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”.

With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison. A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone. However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO). That’s the difference. In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset. This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.content:
We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison.

A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone.

However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO).

That’s the difference.

In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset.

This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: पाकिस्तान से ड्रोन के जरिए हथियार तस्करी के संगठित नेटवर्क का बड़ा खुलासा, बीकानेर पुलिस ने तीन और आरोपियों को किया गिरफ्तार, मुख्य हैंडलर से सीधे संपर्क और सीमा पार सिंडिकेट से जुड़े अहम सुराग मिले In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border. @BSF_India @BSF_Rajasthan @Bikaner_Police @IgpBikaner @FaridkotPolice @FaridkotRange @sgnrpolice @ips_mridul #BikanerPolice #RajasthanPolice #DroneSmuggling #Pakistan #BorderSecurity #IllegalArms #NationalSecurity #IndiaPakistanBorder #CrimeNews #BreakingNews #TINNetwork #DefenceJournalistSahil
tweets.content:
पाकिस्तान से ड्रोन के जरिए हथियार तस्करी के संगठित नेटवर्क का बड़ा खुलासा, बीकानेर पुलिस ने तीन और आरोपियों को किया गिरफ्तार, मुख्य हैंडलर से सीधे संपर्क और सीमा पार सिंडिकेट से जुड़े अहम सुराग मिले

In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border.
@BSF_India @BSF_Rajasthan @Bikaner_Police @IgpBikaner @FaridkotPolice @FaridkotRange @sgnrpolice @ips_mridul
#BikanerPolice #RajasthanPolice #DroneSmuggling #Pakistan #BorderSecurity #IllegalArms #NationalSecurity #IndiaPakistanBorder #CrimeNews #BreakingNews #TINNetwork #DefenceJournalistSahil

tweets.title: "Pakistan's problem is not just KASHMIR. It is India's RISE": Christina Fair If Kashmir was resolved tomorrow, would Pakistan stop confronting India? According to Christina Fair, the answer is a big NO. Fair highlights Pakistan's own military narrative: "Even after losing the 1971 war and losing half of the country, Pakistan claims it was not defeated and remains the only power capable of challenging India's rise in South Asia." Fair argues that Kashmir is only one part of a much larger rivalry. According to her analysis, the core objective is to challenge India's growing power and prevent its rise as a dominant force in South Asia Courtesy: @PeerzadaMoid
tweets.content:
"Pakistan's problem is not just KASHMIR. It is India's RISE": Christina Fair

If Kashmir was resolved tomorrow, would Pakistan stop confronting India? According to Christina Fair, the answer is a big NO.

Fair highlights Pakistan's own military narrative: "Even after losing the 1971 war and losing half of the country, Pakistan claims it was not defeated and remains the only power capable of challenging India's rise in South Asia."

Fair argues that Kashmir is only one part of a much larger rivalry. According to her analysis, the core objective is to challenge India's growing power and prevent its rise as a dominant force in South Asia

Courtesy: @PeerzadaMoid

tweets.title: Around 50 police personnel were reportedly injured today, some critically. A roughly similar number of civilians were also injured. I have a few requests for Delhi Police, the Home Ministry, and other concerned agencies: • Identify those involved who were not students. The agencies have the tools to do this quickly. Please exercise restraint with genuine students and avoid indiscriminate cases against them. • Anyone associated with political parties who participated in unlawful activity should be identified and prosecuted strictly according to law. • Publish an official, verified tally of injuries, arrests, and detentions. There is already a massive wave of propaganda and conflicting claims.
tweets.content:
Around 50 police personnel were reportedly injured today, some critically. A roughly similar number of civilians were also injured.

I have a few requests for Delhi Police, the Home Ministry, and other concerned agencies:

• Identify those involved who were not students. The agencies have the tools to do this quickly. Please exercise restraint with genuine students and avoid indiscriminate cases against them.

• Anyone associated with political parties who participated in unlawful activity should be identified and prosecuted strictly according to law.

• Publish an official, verified tally of injuries, arrests, and detentions. There is already a massive wave of propaganda and conflicting claims.

tweets.title: Come on, be responsible with what you post. We expect maturity from educators, at least. That’s a QRT, or Quick Reaction Team (evident from the vehicle). A QRT responds to any kind of threat that needs to be neutralised and contained immediately, as the name suggests. They are following their SOPs to secure the gate. It doesn’t matter whether protestors are coming through or terrorists. Major installations have multiple QRTs assigned to different yet specific tasks. Kindly cross-check such things before you post, either with your friends in the police/armed forces or with people who have subject-matter knowledge. You know that a post like this can fuel unnecessary distrust among less-informed people.
tweets.content:
Come on, be responsible with what you post. We expect maturity from educators, at least.

That’s a QRT, or Quick Reaction Team (evident from the vehicle). A QRT responds to any kind of threat that needs to be neutralised and contained immediately, as the name suggests.

They are following their SOPs to secure the gate. It doesn’t matter whether protestors are coming through or terrorists.

Major installations have multiple QRTs assigned to different yet specific tasks.

Kindly cross-check such things before you post, either with your friends in the police/armed forces or with people who have subject-matter knowledge.

You know that a post like this can fuel unnecessary distrust among less-informed people.
tweets.author: @elitepredatorss

tweets.title: Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well. That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta. A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
tweets.content:
Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well.

That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta.

A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: 🚨 Breaking News A report circulating from Pakistan claims that on July 8, a Pakistani Army convoy traveling from Balochistan carrying 250 soldiers was ambushed, allegedly resulting in 106 casualties, with 14 additional casualties reported later. The report further alleges that these casualty figures were withheld from the public and media, and were never officially disclosed as face saving. Source: Claims made by Shehbaz Gill. These claims have not been independently verified, and there has been no official confirmation of these figures.
tweets.content:
🚨 Breaking News

A report circulating from Pakistan claims that on July 8, a Pakistani Army convoy traveling from Balochistan carrying 250 soldiers was ambushed, allegedly resulting in 106 casualties, with 14 additional casualties reported later.

The report further alleges that these casualty figures were withheld from the public and media, and were never officially disclosed as face saving.

Source: Claims made by Shehbaz Gill. These claims have not been independently verified, and there has been no official confirmation of these figures.
